extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIČ Primož won the opening ITT by 19 seconds over YATES Simon, using superior climbing power on the steep 2km finale.
tactical_patterns: ROGLIČ took 23 seconds on NIBALI and 19 on YATES in just 2km of climbing, revealing exceptional steep gradient power.
YATES Simon lost 18 seconds to ROGLIČ in flat section but limited climbing losses, signaling changed ITT pacing strategy.
NIBALI surprised with 5 seconds faster than time trial specialist DUMOULIN, exploiting local knowledge from Giro dell'Umbria reconnaissance.

rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: Won all three stage races in 2019 before Giro, showing unprecedented consistency and peak timing
rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: Second place despite losing 18 seconds on flat suggests he sacrificed early watts to preserve climbing legs
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: positive: Beat world TT champion Dumoulin by riding same climb in competition two years prior at Giro dell'Umbria
rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: negative: Former world TT champion beaten by both NIBALI and YATES, suggesting form concerns despite benchmark-setting early start
rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: neutral: Mechanical issues prevented clean ride, rendering performance uninterpretable for form assessment
